finding.title: Canonical queue snapshot and append-only continuity updates describe different current views
finding.summary: operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json is an older canonical snapshot that records M-001 but not later continuity-owned completions. The derived current-view manifest replays append-only records for M-002 through M-004. Both sources can be historically correct; treating either alone as complete current state is unsafe.

claims.claim: The canonical ACTIVE_QUEUE snapshot has blob abb807e1a25d399f301238f6b8cc26d6be10385f and embedded updated_at 2026-07-11T16:51:00-04:00.
claims.falsification_route: Fetch the named path and reject the claim if either blob identity or embedded timestamp differs.

claims.claim: The deterministic continuity-owned current-view manifest records M-002, M-003, and M-004 as completed after the canonical snapshot.
claims.falsification_route: Fetch the manifest at its source commit and verify replayed_updates and materialized_current_view.

claims.claim: Consumers reading only ACTIVE_QUEUE.json can incorrectly infer that no continuity work exists after M-001.
claims.falsification_route: Demonstrate that every current-state consumer automatically replays all owned append-only updates before presenting queue state.

claims.claim: A repository-tree enumerator and deterministic reducer should produce a completeness-checked current view without mutating historical sources.
claims.falsification_route: Reject or revise the proposal if repository-wide enumeration cannot be made deterministic, ownership-safe, and privacy-preserving.

claims.claim: Completeness of the curated replay list remains unproven because this run did not obtain a full repository tree listing for operations/queue-updates.
claims.falsification_route: Enumerate the complete directory tree and show that every continuity_mining update is represented in the reducer input.

resolution_rule.current_state_precedence: canonical snapshot plus all ownership-valid append-only updates ordered by completion time and stable lexical tie-breaker
resolution_rule.historical_truth_rule: A later current view supersedes only present-state interpretation; it does not erase the historical correctness of an earlier snapshot.
resolution_rule.conflict_rule: When two records assert incompatible state for the same item and effective time, retain both, link them explicitly, and mark unresolved until source ordering or authority resolves the conflict.

next_action: Implement a repository-tree enumerator and reducer test that proves every continuity-owned queue update is included exactly once.
